title: Bankroll ablation interpretation
description: Determine which of the five pipeline steps (confidence proxy, deposit, skill gate, weight cap, wealth update) is responsible for the majority of the accuracy gain. Current ablation data shows step-level ΔCRPS but lacks a clear attribution of which step creates the gain vs which adds complexity without benefit.
rationale: Without this analysis, the thesis cannot precisely identify its contribution — the examiner will ask which component matters and which is dead weight.
estimated_effort: 2–3 hours (data exists, needs interpretation module and panel)

title: Real-data replication beyond Elia wind
description: The only real-data benchmark is Elia offshore wind. Replicating on Elia electricity, temperature forecasts, or financial volatility would strengthen generalisability claims.
rationale: A single-dataset result is vulnerable to the criticism that findings are dataset-specific. At least one additional domain would substantially strengthen the argument.
estimated_effort: 4–6 hours per additional dataset (data sourcing + pipeline adaptation + analysis)

title: Regime-conditional claims
description: Break down headline results by regime: early vs late rounds, pre- vs post-perturbation, high vs low volatility. Current results report only aggregate ΔCRPS, which masks regime-dependent performance.
rationale: Aggregate results can hide that the mechanism helps in one regime and hurts in another. Regime-conditional claims are more precise and more defensible.

title: Deposit policy interaction effects
description: Systematically analyse how the deposit policy (fixed, wealth-fraction, Kelly-like) interacts with the skill layer. Current results show fixed deposits yield 21% ΔCRPS but wealth-fraction only ~5% — the interaction mechanism is not fully explained.
rationale: The deposit policy is a key design choice. Understanding the interaction helps practitioners choose the right configuration and strengthens the thesis's practical contribution.

title: Panel size sensitivity (N = 3 vs 6 vs 10 vs 20)
description: Systematically vary the number of forecasters to determine the minimum panel size at which the mechanism reliably beats equal weighting. Current experiments use N = 3, 6, or 10 but do not isolate the panel-size effect.
rationale: Panel size is a practical constraint in real deployments. Knowing the minimum N for the mechanism to work is essential for practitioners and examiners.
estimated_effort: 3–4 hours (new experiment runs at N = 3, 6, 10, 20 + analysis)
